message:
Fix 4 schema↔type drifts: wait-for, search, reservations, release

Align JSON schemas with stabilized Rust types in robot_types.rs:
- wa-robot-wait-for: waited_ms→elapsed_ms, rule_id→pattern, add polls/is_regex
- wa-robot-search: total_matches→total_hits, update search_hit fields
- wa-robot-reservations: add total, id, released_at, status fields
- wa-robot-release: simplify to reservation_id+released

Update typed_client_integration.rs: remove 4 entries from known_drift
(12→8), add schema_match_test! for each fixed schema. All 55 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -675,7 +675,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-upg.8.4","title":"Integrate noise control into status/triage outputs and TUI event feed","description":"# Task: Integrate noise control into UX surfaces

## Goal
Ensure noise control is visible and intuitive in user-facing surfaces.

## Requirements
- CLI:
  - status/triage shows deduped events with counts and last_seen
  - muted events are visible in a separate view
- TUI:
  - event feed shows suppression counts
  - quick actions: mute/unmute
- Notifications:
  - include suppression counts and escalation signals

## Testing
- Output tests for stable formatting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can understand what's happening without being spammed.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8.5","title":"Tests: dedupe/cooldown/mute correctness and determinism","description":"# Task: Tests for noise control

## Goal
Prevent regressions in dedupe/cooldown/mute logic.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for:
  - dedupe within window
  - cooldown suppression
  - mute TTL expiry
  - escalation thresholds

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests lock behavior and make changes intentional.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8.6","title":"E2E: repeated event suppression + escalation + mute","description":"# Task: E2E noise control

## Goal
Validate noise control end-to-end in a realistic runtime.

## Scenario
- Produce an event repeatedly (same key).
- Verify:
  - dedupe collapses events
  - cooldown suppresses notifications
  - escalation triggers after threshold
- Mute the event key.
- Verify muted items no longer notify, but remain visible in muted view.

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - events JSON before/after
  - notification mock server logs (if applicable)

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates high-signal behavior without spam.

 {"id":"wa-upg.9","title":"[EPIC] wa triage: operator dashboard (what needs attention + next actions)","description":"# [EPIC] `wa triage` operator dashboard

## Mission
Provide a single command/view that answers:
- "What needs attention right now?"
- "What should I do next?"

This is the operator experience layer that turns raw events into an actionable queue.

## Why This Matters
wa is powerful but can be opaque. When multiple things happen (crash, gaps, denials, stuck workflows), users need prioritization.

## Scope
- `wa triage` (human):
  - shows prioritized items (incidents/events/workflows/health)
  - shows suggested next commands (quick fixes)
  - supports `--format json`
- Optional TUI integration:
  - triage view filters and quick actions

## Prioritization model
Rank by:
- severity
- age
- repetition
- impact (e.g., number of panes affected)

## Success Criteria
- Users can resolve common issues in under 1 minute using triage.
- Triage output is explainable and consistent.

## Testing
- Unit tests for scoring/prioritization.
- E2E scenario with multiple simultaneous issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa triage` exists with stable JSON schema.
- Triage output includes actionable next steps.
- Tests and E2E validate prioritization and usability.

 {"id":"wa-upg.9.1","title":"Design: triage item schema + scoring model + suggested actions","description":"# Task: Design triage model

## Goal
Define the triage item types, ranking logic, and the "suggested next actions" contract.

## Requirements
- Item types:
  - incidents (crash bundles)
  - unhandled events/detections
  - stuck workflows
  - health warnings (index lag, queue depth)
  - repeated policy denies
- Scoring inputs:
  - severity
  - age
  - repetition
  - blast radius
- Suggested actions:
  - each item includes 1–3 concrete commands (safe first)
  - include explain links (e.g., `wa why ...`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Triage output is deterministic and explainable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:47:02.468250994Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:15:00.365292877Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:15:00.365162405Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.9.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.9.2","title":"Implement wa triage (TTY + --format json)","description":"# Task: Implement `wa triage`

## Goal
Implement the triage command that shows prioritized actionable items.

## Requirements
- Output:
  - TTY: concise list with severity colors and suggested commands
  - JSON: stable schema for automation
- Filtering:
  - `--severity >= <...>`
  - `--only <incidents|events|workflows|health>`
- Ergonomics:
  - default output fits in one screen
  - deep dive with `--verbose`

## Testing
- CLI contract tests for JSON schema.
- Snapshot tests for plain output stability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can run `wa triage` and immediately see what to do next.

     BTreeMap::from([
-        (

-            "Schema uses total_matches; Rust uses total_hits",
-        ),
         (
             "wa-robot-rules-list.json",
             "Schema uses packs/total_rules; Rust uses rules[]",
@@ -136,18 +128,10 @@ fn known_drift_schemas() -> BTreeMap<&'static str, &'static str> {
             "wa-robot-accounts-refresh.json",
             "Schema uses accounts_updated/changes; Rust uses refreshed_count/accounts",
         ),
-        (
-            "wa-robot-reservations.json",
-            "Schema lacks total field; Rust includes total",
-        ),
         (
             "wa-robot-reserve.json",
             "Schema uses flat pane_id/reservation_id; Rust uses nested reservation object",
         ),
-        (
-            "wa-robot-release.json",
-            "Schema uses pane_id+released+more; Rust uses reservation_id+released",
-        ),
         (
             "wa-robot-workflow-run.json",
             "Schema uses steps_planned/policy_decision; Rust uses step_index/message",
@@ -685,6 +669,68 @@ schema_match_test!(
     })
 );
 
+schema_match_test!(
+    schema_wait_for,
+    WaitForData,
+    "wa-robot-wait-for.json",
+    json!({
+        "pane_id": 1,
+        "pattern": "\\$\\s*$",
+        "matched": true,
+        "elapsed_ms": 1200,
+        "polls": 24,
+        "is_regex": true
+    })
+);
+
+schema_match_test!(
+    schema_search,
+    SearchData,
+    "wa-robot-search.json",
+    json!({
+        "query": "error",
+        "results": [{
+            "segment_id": 101,
+            "pane_id": 2,
+            "seq": 5,
+            "captured_at": 1700000000000i64,
+            "score": 2.5,
+            "snippet": "...compile error..."
+        }],
+        "total_hits": 1,
+        "limit": 20
+    })
+);
+
+schema_match_test!(
+    schema_reservations,
+    ReservationsListData,
+    "wa-robot-reservations.json",
+    json!({
+        "reservations": [{
+            "id": 1,
+            "pane_id": 5,
+            "owner_kind": "agent",
+            "owner_id": "codex-1",
+            "reason": "build monitoring",
+            "created_at": 1700000000000i64,
+            "expires_at": 1700000060000i64,
+            "status": "active"
+        }],
+        "total": 1
+    })
+);
+
+schema_match_test!(
+    schema_release,
+    ReleaseData,
+    "wa-robot-release.json",
+    json!({
+        "reservation_id": 7,
+        "released": true
+    })
+);
+
 // ============================================================================
 // Drift detection — document and freeze known schema↔type mismatches
 // ============================================================================
@@ -695,7 +741,7 @@ fn drift_set_is_stable() {

     let known = known_drift_schemas();
-    let expected_count = 12;
+    let expected_count = 8;
     assert_eq!(
         known.len(),
         expected_count,
